title: Junta Kamikaze Drone Strikes Chinese Scam Compound Near Myawaddy — 3 Killed
detail: At approximately 23:30 on March 26, a Myanmar military kamikaze drone struck the 'Kyun Taw' Chinese-operated scam casino compound in Phlu Lay village south of Myawaddy, in an area controlled by the Democratic Karen Buddhist Army (DKBA). Three Chinese nationals were killed instantly. The strike was notable as it targeted a Chinese compound in a non-junta-controlled zone, reflecting the complexity of the Myawaddy border area where multiple armed actors operate alongside Chinese-run online scam operations. Myawaddy and its surroundings have been a flashpoint for inter-factional conflict between KNU, DKBA, and other groups, compounded by the presence of Chinese scam compounds that have become a source of regional tension and diplomatic concern.

title: UK Imposes Sanctions on Triad Leader Running Myawaddy Scam Compound
detail: On March 26, the United Kingdom imposed targeted sanctions on Wan Kuok Koi (known as 'Broken Tooth'), a Macau-based organized crime figure and triad leader who owns and operates a major scam compound in DKBA-controlled territory near Myawaddy, Karen State. The Prince Group (Cambodia-based) was also sanctioned. The UK sanctions followed similar designations by the US and Canada. Justice For Myanmar and anti-trafficking organizations had campaigned for the measures for months. The sanctions target the financial infrastructure enabling large-scale online fraud and human trafficking operations that have exploited thousands of people across Southeast Asia, many of them trafficked from Thailand, China, and other countries.
